nodes.title: Separate query, popup, status, active option, and result state
nodes.why: Autocomplete has several independent UI states. Keeping status separate from results prevents loading, no-results, and error from collapsing into one ambiguous branch.
nodes.alternative: Derive everything from query and results length.
nodes.tradeoff: Explicit state adds fields, but it makes interview tests and edge states easier to reason about.

nodes.title: Close and reset on empty input before scheduling async work
nodes.why: Whitespace-only input should not leave stale suggestions or loading UI open. Returning early also avoids unnecessary timers.
nodes.alternative: Let the search function return an empty list for blank queries.
nodes.tradeoff: The early branch is more explicit and avoids flicker, but it duplicates a few reset calls.

nodes.title: Guard async results with request identity
nodes.why: Clearing a debounce timer stops work that has not started yet; it does not cancel an already-started Promise. The request id prevents an older slow response from overwriting newer results.
nodes.alternative: Use AbortController for a real fetch request.
nodes.tradeoff: A request id works for fake async and uncancellable Promises, while AbortController can also save network work when fetch supports it.

nodes.title: Map async success to success or empty explicitly
nodes.why: No-results is a successful search with an empty payload, not an error. This distinction keeps user feedback and tests deterministic.
nodes.alternative: Use only a boolean loading flag and render no-results whenever results are empty.
nodes.tradeoff: A status enum is more verbose, but it avoids showing no-results during loading or after errors.

nodes.title: Clean debounce timer and mark the effect stale
nodes.why: Cleanup handles both rerenders and unmount. The timer cleanup removes pending debounce work, and the ignore flag stops late async continuations from updating state.
nodes.alternative: Store every timer globally and clear them from outside the component.
nodes.tradeoff: Effect-local cleanup is scoped and easy to test, but each effect must own its own resources carefully.

nodes.title: Own outside-close behavior at the component boundary
nodes.why: The component needs a document-level listener to close when the pointer starts outside the autocomplete root while keeping inside option interactions intact.
nodes.tradeoff: A document listener needs cleanup, but it avoids blur timing bugs and works for pointer interactions outside the input.

nodes.title: Keep focus on the input while activeIndex changes
nodes.why: Combobox keyboard navigation should preserve input focus and expose the active option through aria-activedescendant.
nodes.alternative: Move DOM focus into listbox options.
nodes.tradeoff: Input-owned focus requires more ARIA wiring, but typing, Escape, and screen-reader context stay coherent.

nodes.title: Selecting an option writes the value and closes the popup
nodes.why: Selection is a distinct transition: the chosen label becomes the query, results are cleared, popup state resets, and focus returns to the input.
nodes.alternative: Leave the results open after selection.
nodes.tradeoff: Closing is the expected interview behavior here; keeping results open can be useful for multi-select variants.

nodes.title: Wire the input as the combobox owner
nodes.why: The input owns the editable value while listbox options provide suggestions. The ARIA attributes connect open state, listbox id, and active option id.
nodes.alternative: Render a plain input and unordered list with no relationship.
nodes.tradeoff: ARIA wiring adds attributes, but it makes keyboard navigation and assistive technology behavior testable.
